seoTitle: How to Start a Thread in Slack Quickly
metaDescription: Reply in a Slack thread to keep side conversations organized, and use the Threads tab to catch up on everything you're following.
intro: Keeping a busy Slack channel readable often comes down to using threads instead of replying directly in the main feed. This guide covers starting a thread, broadcasting key replies back to the channel, and catching up using the Threads tab.

steps.heading: Hover over a message
steps.body: Move your cursor over the message you want to reply to in order to reveal the message options.

steps.heading: Click Reply in Thread
steps.body: Select the speech bubble icon to open the thread panel on the right side of your screen.

steps.heading: Type your reply
steps.body: Enter your response in the thread's input field and press Send.

steps.heading: Broadcast to the channel if needed
steps.body: Check 'Also send to channel' before sending if the reply is important enough for everyone to see immediately.

steps.heading: Catch up using the Threads tab
steps.body: Open the Threads section in your left sidebar to review all active conversations you're following.

faq.question: Do all replies to a message automatically start a thread?
faq.answer: Yes, once someone replies using the thread option, subsequent replies are grouped under that same thread.

faq.question: Can I stop following a thread I'm no longer interested in?
faq.answer: Yes, you can unfollow a thread from its options to stop receiving notifications for it.

faq.question: Will thread replies show up in the main channel feed?
faq.answer: Only a short preview shows in the channel unless you choose to broadcast the specific reply.

faq.question: Can I mention someone directly inside a thread?
faq.answer: Yes, use the @ shortcut inside the thread just as you would in a regular channel message.
